ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job performance entry */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being tracked */,
  "wait_time" REAL /* Time spent by the job in waiting state */,
  "turn_around_time" REAL /* Time taken for the job to complete from submission */,
  "exe_time" REAL /* Time taken for job execution */,
  "cpu_utilization" REAL /* CPU utilization during job execution */,
  "memory_utilization" REAL /* Memory utilization during job execution */,
  "io_operations" INTEGER /* Number of I/O operations performed */,
  "network_usage" REAL /* Network usage during job execution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_expansion" (
  "expansion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job expansion entry */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being tracked */,
  "no_of_expansion" INTEGER /* Number of times the job expanded */,
  "expansion_time" REAL /* Time taken for job expansion */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for job expansion */,
  "additional_resources" TEXT /* Additional resources required for expansion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("expansion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_shrinkage" (
  "shrinkage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job shrinkage entry */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being tracked */,
  "no_of_shrinkage" INTEGER /* Number of times the job shrunk */,
  "shrinkage_time" REAL /* Time taken for job shrinkage */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for job shrinkage */,
  "released_resources" TEXT /* Resources released during shrinkage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shrinkage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource */,
  "resource_name" TEXT /* Name of the resource (e.g., CPU, memory) */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of the resource (e.g., computing, storage) */,
  "available_units" INTEGER /* Number of available units of the resource */,
  "total_units" INTEGER /* Total units of the resource */,
  "used_units" INTEGER /* Units of the resource currently in use */,
  "last_updated" REAL /* Time when the resource information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_resource_allocation" (
  "allocation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each allocation entry */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being allocated resources */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource being allocated */,
  "allocated_units" INTEGER /* Number of units of the resource allocated */,
  "start_time" REAL /* Time when the resource allocation started */,
  "end_time" REAL /* Time when the resource allocation ended */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the resource allocation (e.g., allocated, released) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("allocation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_resource_allocation_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_submission" (
  "submission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job submission */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being submitted */,
  "submission_time" REAL /* Time of job submission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user submitting the job */,
  "submission_node" TEXT /* Node from which the job was submitted */,
  "estimated_start_time" REAL /* Estimated start time of the job */,
  "actual_start_time" REAL /* Actual start time of the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("submission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_submission_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
